query: Glideo: Screen recordings that edit themselves alternative

results.title: Screencastify Alternatives: Better Options for Pros | Kommodo
results.content:
With the screen recorder, you can highlight your device's screen, your webcam, or both. Then you can share recordings and monitor viewers' engagement.

Using the video editor, you can modify screen recordings and imported videos. From trimming and merging to reordering and annotating, the editor has a solid toolset.

### Who is Screencastify for?

This screen recording software has three primary user bases. It's best for: [...] Tables can be generated with headings and text alignment option

#### 1. Kommodo

Like Screencastify, Kommodo is a free screen recorder that captures your screen or webcam so you can share async communications with clients, colleagues, and students. But Kommodo can do more than just record video via a Chrome extension.

Kommodo's macOS desktop app automatically creates AI-powered transcripts from your videos. You can edit them by re-recording words and phrases to produce error-free screen recordings that are easy to understand.

You can embed or share screen recordings anywhere, with optional password protection and link expiration. Viewers can leave comments on your videos, making it easy to get team feedback, client approval, or student questions. [...] Best for: Professionals who need an all-in-one recording, editing, and sharing solution with an enterprise option.

Pros:

 Includes editing tools for trimming videos, removing filler words, and creating custom thumbnails
 Provides viewer and engagement insights so you can see what's working

Cons:

 Free plan includes Loom branding and limits videos to 5 minutes

#### 7. Movavi

Movavi is a video suite that includes a screen recorder, editing tools, and an effects library. With the desktop app, you can capture calls and presentations, make simple edits, and add annotations.

Best for: Video creators who need a tool that has editing features and integrates with a production suite.

Pros:

results.title: Screen Studio Alternatives: 4 Tools for Teams Who Need More
results.content:
HowdyGo Sandbox UI

Editing is what a Screen Studio user notices first. Every step in HowdyGo's capture is real DOM, so you can change the text after recording. Spot a wrong heading or a customer's name you didn't mean to share, and you fix it in the editor without recording anything again.

After a few edits in a normal session, you're usually further ahead than you'd be with a video tool, even though video looks like the simpler format up front.

The editor has an AI agent that takes a captured demo and writes the first draft for you: the storyboard, the annotations, and the links between screens, all populated. You refine it in a chat until it's correct, usually within a couple of minutes. It can add AI voice narration too, with no recording required. [...] Tella treats your recording like a document. You edit the transcript directly, and cutting a word from the text cuts it from the video. Misspoke a customer name? Fix it without re-recording. A teleprompter and speaker notes take the heat out of longer demos, the kind where you'd otherwise tape a script to a second monitor.

Every recording lands on a hosted page automatically, with a shareable link and embed code ready to drop into a landing page or a CRM. Reactions and timestamped comments come along for the ride. You don't end up holding a video file you have to upload to YouTube and then track down the URL later. [...] Sign inBook a demoStart 14-day trial

# Screen Studio Alternatives: 4 Tools for Teams Who Need More Than a Video

Daniel Engelke profile picture

Daniel Engelke

Co-founder

9 min read

Table of Contents

 What to look for in a Screen Studio alternative
 Tella
 Loom
 Arcade
 HowdyGo
 Screen Studio alternatives compared
 Which one is right for your job
 Conclusion

Screen Studio makes recordings that look produced, and for plenty of people that is its main value proposition. The trouble starts the first time you need to update one. Once you stop recording, everything on the screen is frozen: a typo in a heading or a price that went stale last quarter, every one of them is another take.

results.title: Top 5 Screen Studio Alternatives (2026) - DEV Community
results.content:
## Borumi

Borumi is the strongest all-around alternative to Screen Studio if you want more structure, more editing flexibility, and support for both macOS and Windows. Instead of forcing you into one long recording, it lets you plan and record scene by scene, which makes educational videos, product demos, and tutorials much easier to produce. It also combines recording and editing in one app, so you can go from idea to finished video without needing a separate editor. It also comes with smooth zoom-in effects, beautiful layouts and much more to make your video look professional with minimal effort.

## Loom

results.title: Best Screen Recorder in 2025 (BETTER than Loom!) - Record Yourself and Your Screen At the Same Time
results.content:
But for me, the real magic is in the editing part. It's not too advanced but it also allows a lot 
of customizations which competitors don't offer. Loom for starters doesn't offer much of editing 
except trimming and stitching the clips. You can't change the layout, you 
can't even move the window with your face when it accidentally covers 
important elements on your screen share. This happened to me too many times, and 
I had to re-record parts of of the video. But in Tella it's very easy to fix it because 
you can move the window with your face while you edit the clip, or even choose the layout 
without your face for a particular clip. Tella is perfect if you want to simultaneously 
capture yourself and show your screen. You can record the video with your face and then [...] change the format of the window when you edit it, you can choose it to be a smaller rounded window, or square, or rectangular window on 
the right side or on the left side. You can even switch from screen 
sharing to a full-faced avatar, and for that select the 'Full 
Talking Head' layout option. Another feature I haven't seen anywhere else - you can customize your background from 
Tella's selection or upload your own. I really like those animated backgrounds that 
are slowly changing colors as the video goes on. Other tweaks include border style, 
you can choose these rounded corners, or a few styles of your screen 
share, with or without shadow. If you are wondering, what does this 
camera mirroring do, check it out! You can also crop the screen. [...] zoom, as well as the speed of it. You can edit the clips you recorded in the 
brower using a very intuitive interface. So no need to take your videos to a 
third-party video editing software which usually has a very steep learning 
curve, plus it often comes at an extra cost. In Tella editing is straightforward. 
Trimming is essential, and Tella makes it easy to cut the start, end, or 
even even the middle segments. You just delete the bit that you don't need, and Tella 
automatically stitches the rest of the video. So to wrap it up, Tella has a lot of 
advantages over other similar tools. There is NO need for additional software, you 
can record and edit the videos in the same tool. Another advantage is instant sharing

results.title: The best screen recording software in 2026
results.content:
Loom pricing: Free for 25 videos of up to five minutes. Business plans start at $18/month/user with unlimited videos and recording length.

Jumpshare is a solid Loom alternative that works across most devices and platforms. I love that once you finish recording, the secure link is automatically copied to your clipboard. The free plan is limited to recordings under one minute, but paid plans are reasonable and include features to edit your recording and add CTAs, transcription, and captions.

## Best screen recording software for advanced video editing

### Camtasia(Windows, Mac)

Camtasia pros:

 Simple, easy-to-use capture
 Robust editing features

Camtasia cons:

 High upfront cost
